issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
When targeting Android 13 or higher, posting a permission requires holding the `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` permission (usage from com.bumptech.glide.request.target.NotificationTarget)
A newer version of com.android.application than 8.9.1 is available: 8.10.0. (There is also a newer version of 8.9.𝑥 available, if upgrading to 8.10.0 is difficult: 8.9.3)
A newer version of com.android.application than 8.9.1 is available: 8.10.0. (There is also a newer version of 8.9.𝑥 available, if upgrading to 8.10.0 is difficult: 8.9.3)
A newer version of com.android.application than 8.9.1 is available: 8.10.0. (There is also a newer version of 8.9.𝑥 available, if upgrading to 8.10.0 is difficult: 8.9.3)
It will always be more efficient to use more specific change events if you can. Rely on `notifyDataSetChanged` as a last resort.
Possible overdraw: Root element paints background `?attr/colorSurface` with a theme that also paints a background (inferred theme is `@style/Theme.TopacAdemy_android`)

Number formatting does not take into account locale settings. Consider using `String.format` instead.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

Hardcoded string "Ощущается как", should use `@string` resource
Hardcoded string "Влажность", should use `@string` resource
Hardcoded string "Ветер", should use `@string` resource
Hardcoded string "Осадки", should use `@string` resource
Consider replacing `android:drawableLeft` with `android:drawableStart="@android:drawable/ic_dialog_info"` to better support right-to-left layouts
Consider replacing `android:drawableLeft` with `android:drawableStart="@android:drawable/ic_dialog_info"` to better support right-to-left layouts
